\ Executive Board Member*

Ultimate Orienteer Series to Begin in March

It's not too late to compete in the Ultimate Orienteer series. With 3 competitions remaining, you can still satisfy the 3-meet minimum for a shot at becoming the “Ultimate Orienteer.” The last three events are:

Score-O' - April 19 at Woodland Park Classic-O' - May 10 at Cle Elum Ridge
* Long-O' - June 1 at a location to be announced

Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race

The Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race will begin at St. Edward State Park in Kenmore on August 30. Please join our organizing team and help us make this a great event. Volunteers so far include:

__PH_0__ - Debbie Newell Course Planners - Erik Nachtrieb, Dave Tallent
__PH_2__ - Jennifer Knowles First Aid Coordinator - Bill Allemann
* Day-of-event help - Don Atkinson, Eugen Groff

Other volunteer opportunities include:

Food Awards
Sponsorship Equipment manager
Volunteer coordination Map production
Publicity Timing/results
First aid T-shirt

If you would like to help in any of these areas, please contact Debbie at 425-488-3691 (dnewel@verizone.net). We need your help to put on a quality event and your participation will be greatly appreciated. For more information about the race, see the club's website.

Pacific Northwest Orienteering Festival 2003

If you're planning to attend the Pacific Northwest Orienteering Festival this summer, please consider volunteering to help out. The schedule of events appears below.

DateEventLocation
June 21-22Classic-OTeanaway (COC)
June 23Short-OFishtrap Lake, e. Spokane
June 24Goat eventRiverside State Park, Spokane
June 25Trail-ORiverside State Park
June 25Night-ORiverside State Park
June 27Sprint-OThorn Creek, McCall ID
June 28Classic-OBear Basin, McCall ID
June 29US Relay ChampsPonderosa St. Park, ID

To volunteer at Cascade's event, please contact Jennifer Knowles (253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com). You can also help out at any of the other events by contacting the appropriate person for that event. See the event website (http://velichko.home.mindspring.com/PNWOF2003) for additional information.

E-Punch Decision

At its March board meeting, COC's Board of Directors approved the purchase of the Sportident electronic punching system. After a demonstration of the system at the March 8 Seward Park event, it was clear that the chief advantages of the system are accuracy and speed of results. Depending on when the equipment arrives, we plan to use it at some of our spring events, as well as at the PNWOF event in Cle Elum, the Six Degrees of Navigation adventure race, and at all of next season's WIOL events. Jennifer Knowles, in addition to her duties as club president, has agreed to be the E-punch Coordinator for at least the next 2 years. Thanks, Jennifer, for taking on this critical role.

[Image: Black and white photo of an orienteering control flag hidden in dense, leafless winter vegetation and brush. Text below reads: "A control lurks in the vegetation at Ft. Steilacoom Park (photo: Debbie Newell; camera: Rick Breseman)"]

Spring Skills Clinic a Success

Sixteen people braved blustery spring weather for a day of orienteering workshops on March 22 at Lynndale Park. The classroom at Lynndale was smaller than expected, so this turned out to be a very full house! The participants ranged from absolute beginners to people with quite a bit of experience. Doug Sprugel started the day with a discussion of basic and intermediate level skills that included some lively discussion about route choices. This was followed by practice with compass skills on a precision pacing and bearing course set up by Eric Bone. Bill Cusworth gave a clear overview of the effort and jobs that go into making each orienteering event succeed or fail. After lunch, Eric talked about strategies for relocating when you lose contact with the map or can't find that darn control, then sent everyone out in pairs on complementary maps for a relocation exercise. The day ended with a session on course design presented by Katie Sprugel, focusing on local meets. Everyone got a chance to design some hypothetical legs at Magnuson Park and get them critiqued. A good time appeared to be had by all (and the forecast rain didn't happen)!

We had to turn a few people away because the clinic was full, so we will consider doing another one in the fall if there is sufficient interest.

Thanks to Katie for organizing the workshop and Eric, Doug, Katie and Bill for presenting. Jim Siscel made arrangements for the room at Lynndale. Thanks also to Bruce McAlister for bringing some great home baked treats.

Katie Sprugel

Local Students Attend US Interscholastic Champs

Several students from the Northwest attended the US Interscholastic Orienteering Champs in Georgia in January. The following students had the opportunity to compete in the open, runnable forests of Sweetwater State Park near Atlanta:

NameSchoolResult
Leif AndersonBremerton High School1st, varsity boys
Hilary DyerThomas Jefferson H.S.2nd, varsity girls
Will EngerInt'l Comm. School5th, varsity boys
Kyle Haddad-FondaLakesidemispunch
Ryan BresemanLeota Jr. H.S.35th, jr. varsity boys
Kelsey BresemanWoodinville Montessori2nd, girls m.s.
Matt SkorinaSacajawea Orienteers7th, boys m.s.

Congratulations to everyone who participated in the event. Next year's Interscholastic Championships is scheduled for the end of March in Virginia.

The Ultimate Orienteer - Current Standings

> Meets in the 2003 Ultimate Orienteer Series
> 1 - Seward Park Short-O (March 8) • 2 - Lynndale Park Night-O (March 29) • 3 - Woodland Park Score-O (April 19) • 4 - Cle Elum Ridge Classic-O (May 10) • 5 - Long-O, location TBA (June 01)

> Ultimate Orienteer Scoring System
> The winner of each event receives 1000 points. Everyone else receives a percentage of 1000 points based on their time compared to that of the winner. For example, if the winner has a time of 25 minutes and your time is 50 minutes, you will receive 500 points. Your best 3 scores are combined for your total score.

block_index.text: [Image: Cascade Orienteering Club logo featuring a map grid square with a compass star and directional letters N, S, E, W. To the right, the large bold text reads "Bearing 315".]
The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club Second Quarter 2003 www.CascadeOC.org Page 1

President – Jennifer Knowles, 253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com Vice President – Eugen Groff, 206-985-2198, groffmarek@earthlink.net Training/Volunteer Coordinator – Eric Bone, 206-634-2279, eric.bone@attbi.com Treasurer – Katie Sprugel, 206-365-8742, sprugels@att.net Secretary – Ardis Dull, 206-364-4062, ardisdull@juno.com Mapping Coordinator – Don Denbo, 206-366-0624, dwd6@mindspring.com Equipment Coordinator – Don Atkinson, 425-488-0270, dona@atmos.washington.edu Newsletter Editor – Debbie Newell, 425-488-3691, dnewel@verizon.net Membership Coordinator – Jim Siscel, 425-778-7202, jsiscel@cmc.net Publicity/Promotion Coordinator – vacant Land Use Coordinator – Steve Knowles, 253-845-6588 Technical Coordinator – Bill Cusworth, 206-271-0652, cusworth@chem.washington.edu E-punch Coordinator – Jennifer Knowles Patrick Kelly, 206-760-9554, patandlaureen@onebox.com
Ultimate Orienteer Series to Begin in March

It's not too late to compete in the Ultimate Orienteer series. With 3 competitions remaining, you can still satisfy the 3-meet minimum for a shot at becoming the “Ultimate Orienteer.” The last three events are:
Score-O' - April 19 at Woodland Park Classic-O' - May 10 at Cle Elum Ridge Long-O' - June 1 at a location to be announced
Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race

The Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race will begin at St. Edward State Park in Kenmore on August 30. Please join our organizing team and help us make this a great event. Volunteers so far include:

Event Director - Debbie Newell Course Planners - Erik Nachtrieb, Dave Tallent Communications Director - Jennifer Knowles First Aid Coordinator - Bill Allemann Day-of-event help - Don Atkinson, Eugen Groff
Food Awards Sponsorship Equipment manager Volunteer coordination Map production Publicity Timing/results First aid T-shirt

If you would like to help in any of these areas, please contact Debbie at 425-488-3691 (dnewel@verizone.net). We need your help to put on a quality event and your participation will be greatly appreciated. For more information about the race, see the club's website.
If you're planning to attend the Pacific Northwest Orienteering Festival this summer, please consider volunteering to help out. The schedule of events appears below.

To volunteer at Cascade's event, please contact Jennifer Knowles (253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com). You can also help out at any of the other events by contacting the appropriate person for that event. See the event website (http://velichko.home.mindspring.com/PNWOF2003) for additional information.

At its March board meeting, COC's Board of Directors approved the purchase of the Sportident electronic punching system. After a demonstration of the system at the March 8 Seward Park event, it was clear that the chief advantages of the system are accuracy and speed of results. Depending on when the equipment arrives, we plan to use it at some of our spring events, as well as at the PNWOF event in Cle Elum, the Six Degrees of Navigation adventure race, and at all of next season's WIOL events. Jennifer Knowles, in addition to her duties as club president, has agreed to be the E-punch Coordinator for at least the next 2 years. Thanks, Jennifer, for taking on this critical role.

[Image: Black and white photo of an orienteering control flag hidden in dense, leafless winter vegetation and brush. Text below reads: "A control lurks in the vegetation at Ft. Steilacoom Park (photo: Debbie Newell; camera: Rick Breseman)"]

Sixteen people braved blustery spring weather for a day of orienteering workshops on March 22 at Lynndale Park. The classroom at Lynndale was smaller than expected, so this turned out to be a very full house! The participants ranged from absolute beginners to people with quite a bit of experience. Doug Sprugel started the day with a discussion of basic and intermediate level skills that included some lively discussion about route choices. This was followed by practice with compass skills on a precision pacing and bearing course set up by Eric Bone. Bill Cusworth gave a clear overview of the effort and jobs that go into making each orienteering event succeed or fail. After lunch, Eric talked about strategies for relocating when you lose contact with the map or can't find that darn control, then sent everyone out in pairs on complementary maps for a relocation exercise. The day ended with a session on course design presented by Katie Sprugel, focusing on local meets. Everyone got a chance to design some hypothetical legs at Magnuson Park and get them critiqued. A good time appeared to be had by all (and the forecast rain didn't happen)!
We had to turn a few people away because the clinic was full, so we will consider doing another one in the fall if there is sufficient interest.

Thanks to Katie for organizing the workshop and Eric, Doug, Katie and Bill for presenting. Jim Siscel made arrangements for the room at Lynndale. Thanks also to Bruce McAlister for bringing some great home baked treats.
Local Students Attend US Interscholastic Champs

Several students from the Northwest attended the US Interscholastic Orienteering Champs in Georgia in January. The following students had the opportunity to compete in the open, runnable forests of Sweetwater State Park near Atlanta:
Congratulations to everyone who participated in the event. Next year's Interscholastic Championships is scheduled for the end of March in Virginia.

Meet Director: Jennifer Knowles Courses: Jon Campbell Registration: Don Denbo (WIOL), Sandy Kish Miller, Tori Campbell Starts: Pat Kelly, Mark Howlett, cadets from Curtis Finish: Eugen Groff, Steve Knowles, Ardis Dull, Don Atkinson, cadet from Curtis Pickup: Jon & Tori Campbell
Meet Director: Ginger Stratton Courses: Don Atkinson

Course 4 (6.6 K, 19 controls) - Winter O' Series Championships

Meet Director: Peter Golde Course Setter: Jennifer Knowles, Steve Knowles Start: Philippe Nicolle, Pat Kelly Finish: Jon Campbell, Eugen Groff, Don Atkinson Registration: Eric Bone, Terry Farrah, Beth Golde E-Punch System: Jennifer Knowles, Debbie Newell, Tori Campbell Marker Pickup: Dean Vergillo, Pat Kelly

Meet Director: Debbie Newell Courses: Eric Bone Registration: Eugen Groff Start: Bjorn Freeman-Benson, Dave Tallent Finish: A.J. Ritter, Bill Allemann, Robin Clark Instruction: Dave Tallent, Kean Williams Maker Pickup: Debbie Newell, Dave Tallent
